name: Alan Dawson
abstract: Alan Dawson (July 14, 1929 – February 23, 1996) was an American jazz drummer and percussion teacher based in Boston.

about_quotes.speaker: Art Farmer
about_quotes.evidence: That’s when I started making records under my own name in the summer of ’53. Then in the fall of ’53, I went over to Europe with Lionel’s band. It was a great band with Clifford Brown, Gigi Gryce, --you know, the cats, --James Cleveland, Quincy Jones, Mark Montgomery, Alan Dawson, really good player
about_quotes.doc_title: Art Farmer — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Don Alias
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewer asks 'And Alan Dawson. Did you have a defined goal at that time?' The interviewee responds about his general attitude towards ambition and playing, without mentioning any personal interaction, relationship, or specific knowledge of Alan Dawson.
about_quotes.doc_title: Don Alias interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 6, 2002

about_quotes.speaker: Jackie Williams
about_quotes.evidence: And that’s when I was at Wally’s, that’s when I first, with Eugene Smith, I first met Alan Dawson, the great drummer from New England.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jackie Williams interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, October 18, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Dave Brubeck
about_quotes.evidence: His name is Alan Dawson. You get him. You’ll have what you want.” So I called him. “Come to Wilton and rehearse,” and Jack Six to come to Wilton. I started playing one tune. They played it through. Jack put his bass down. Alan stood up. They came close to each other and embraced, without saying a wo
about_quotes.doc_title: Dave Brubeck — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Konitz Lee
about_quotes.evidence: Bill, Niels Henning, and Alan Dawson... we ended up playing as two separate trios and just under the contract we played that last tune as a quartet... enjoyed playing with those two guys a lot.
about_quotes.doc_title: Konitz Lee — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Sam Most
about_quotes.evidence: I did an album, I went to Japan with Ray Brown, Hank Jones and Alan Dawson. And we did a recording over there
about_quotes.doc_title: Sam Most interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, February 15, 1999
